title: Kathmandu - The Kathmandu Post
summary: Maternity hospital to manage medical waste of KMC’s urban health promotion centres By Post Report Along with private health facilities, some state-run health facilities also mix up hazardous medical waste with household waste. RSP’s Dol Prasad Aryal wins Kathmandu-9 By Post Report Aryal secures victory by a margin of nearly 30,000 votes over Congress rival. RSP’s Pukar Bam wins Kathmandu-4 By Post
content:
Maternity hospital to manage medical waste of KMC’s urban health promotion centres By Post Report Along with private health facilities, some state-run health facilities also mix up hazardous medical waste with household waste. RSP’s Dol Prasad Aryal wins Kathmandu-9 By Post Report Aryal secures victory by a margin of nearly 30,000 votes over Congress rival. RSP’s Pukar Bam wins Kathmandu-4 By Post Report Nepali Congress and UML candidates trail far behind in final tally. RSP’s Raju Nath Pandey defeats Kulman Ghising in Kathmandu-3 By Post Report Rastriya Swatantra Party candidate secures victory with 18,757 votes; Ujyalo Nepal Party chair finishes second. RSP’s Ranju Darshana wins Kathmandu-1 By Post Report Nepali Congress candidate Prabal Thapa finishes second in final tally. Ranju Darshana close to victory in Kathmandu-1 as counting nears completion By Post Report RSP candidate holds commanding lead with around 8,000 votes left to be counted. RSP widens lead in Kathmandu-10 By Post Report Pradeep Bista ahead by 5,000 votes. RSP widens lead in Kathmandu-7 By Post Report Ganesh Parajuli strengthens early advantage with over 4,600 votes Rastriya Swatantra Party leads in both Bhaktapur constituencies By Post Report Rukesh Ranjit and Rajiv Khatri ahead in Bhaktapur-1 and Bhaktapur-2, respectively. Biraj Bhakta Shrestha surges ahead in Kathmandu-8 By Post Report Rastriya Swatantra Party candidate maintains strong lead over Congress and UML rivals. Raju Pandey takes lead over Kulman Ghising in Kathmandu-3 By Post Report Pandey surpasses Ghising, who held early lead, in preliminary vote count. Rastriya Swatantra Party’s Pukar Bam takes early lead in Kathmandu-4 By Post Report Bam ahead of UML’s Rajan Bhattarai as initial vote count is released. Rastriya Swatantra Party’s Pradeep Bista leads in Kathmandu-10 By Post Report Bista takes early lead as vote counting begins in the constituency. Sunil KC takes early lead in Kathmandu-2 By Post Report Rastriya Swatantra Party candidate ahead of UML’s Maniram Phuyal in preliminary vote count. Rastriya Swatantra Party’s Ranju Darshana maintains lead in Kathmandu-1 By Post Report Darshana ahead of Nepali Congress candidate Prabal Thapa as vote counting progresses. Car driver arrested for assaulting pedestrian in Kathmandu By Post Report Traffic police take action after viral video shows driver punching pedestrian. Fire at hotel in Durbar Marg brought under control By Post Report Blaze broke out on the hotel’s upper floor in the morning; no casualties reported. Poor waste management turns Pashupati area parking lot into a mess By Aarya Chand Trash from pilgrims’ cooking and washing piles up as the Pashupati Area Development Trust finds it hard to enforce rules. Kathmandu mayor Balendra Shah resigns By Post Report Shah plans to contest from Jhapa-5 to challenge Oli. Kathmandu metropolis asks 107 non-operational schools to come into contact By Post Report Education department warns of cancellation of approval if schools fail to respond within 15 days.
